category_title: Low-achieving vocational students in a computer maintenance class
minimal_context: The student is sitting at a PC after a two-session exam and is asked to post their circuit drawings from the exam to their blog.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Now that the exam is over, please post your answers for questions 1 and 2, the drawing circuits, to your blog.

STUDENT: We just had an exam for the first two sessions. I feel exhausted.

TUTOR: You still need to complete this assignment today.

STUDENT: I feel annoyed. I don't want to do another assignment right now because I don't like to work under pressure.

TUTOR: Please open your blog on the PC and start drawing the circuits.

STUDENT: (Opens a new browser tab) I'm not doing it. I'm going to open YouTube and then play Counterstrike with the others.

options.A: Conduct a face-to-face evaluation with the student, offering positive explanation and appreciation for their previous exam work.
options.B: Warn the student that if they do not complete the blog assignment, they risk losing the opportunity to play Counterstrike.
options.C: Ask the student to propose alternative activities, prompting them to share their own ideas and examples for the blog assignment.
options.D: Help the student understand the circuit questions and continuously direct their attention back to the blog assignment.
